what_it_does: TypeScript declaration file for a React component named Glyphic. Declares the GlyphicProps interface (extending React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>) and exports the Glyphic function component plus a default export.
observations: GlyphicProps fields: form, symbol, fill, ringColor, symbolColor, texture, motion, depth, size, value — each documented with a JSDoc comment.
form is a string-literal union: none | triangle | square | pentagon | hex | septagon | octagon | diamond | circle.
fill union: none | paper | wash | tint; depth union: flat | d1..d6 | normal; motion union of 8 named ambient/entrance modes; texture union of 8 named surface patterns.
ringColor, symbolColor, and value are typed as plain string (colour-token names / allocated values), not literal unions.
Top-of-file JSDoc describes the 'ConceptV Glyphic' as a perfect-square universal compositional mark: outer ring (n-gon form) plus inner symbol, with whole-unit slots size/motion/depth/value.

Docstring states geometry/symbols/colour/texture/motion/depth all resolve via window.CV_GLYPHIC.compose, and this component is 'the React socket around that one renderer, not a second implementation'.
value drives colour through the 'active meaning profile'.
standouts: The docstring's framing of the component as a 'React socket' around a single window.CV_GLYPHIC.compose renderer rather than a reimplementation.
The 'allocated value drives colour through the active meaning profile' coupling of a semantic value to colour resolution.
conventions: Single-source / reference-not-copy: declares a socket over one renderer rather than embedding rendering logic (matches the project's 'one home' directive).
Heavy JSDoc on every prop — typed surface is self-documenting.
String-literal unions for closed vocabularies (form/fill/depth/motion/texture); open string for colour-token names and values.
notes: The .d.ts type contract for the Glyphic compositional-mark component — defines its prop vocabulary and frames it as a thin React socket over the CV_GLYPHIC renderer.
questions: What is window.CV_GLYPHIC.compose and where does it live (only referenced, not defined here)?
What are the valid colour-token names and 'value' strings (typed only as string)?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the docstring describes a compositional mark component and the interface declares exactly the props that mark would need.
apparent_themes: Compositional design primitive (ring + symbol + whole-unit slots)
Single-source rendering (one compose renderer, React as socket)
Semantic-to-visual mapping (value → colour via meaning profile)
intention_signals: Aims to expose a typed, well-documented React surface for a universal compositional glyph mark while keeping the actual rendering single-sourced in one renderer.
novelty: The 'Glyphic' concept — a perfect-square mark built from independent meaning-bearing parts (n-gon ring form + inner symbol + spaces) with an allocated value driving colour.
contribution: Contributes the typed prop vocabulary and conceptual definition of the Glyphic compositional mark — the closed vocabularies for form/fill/texture/motion/depth and the value→meaning-profile colour coupling.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript .d.ts declaring the GlyphicProps interface and Glyphic React component, a perfect-square compositional mark with ring form, inner symbol, and slots for fill, colour, texture, motion, depth, size, and an allocated value.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the typed contract for a universal compositional glyph mark rendered through a single CV_GLYPHIC.compose renderer.
